items.avoid_when: The topic is only metaphorically about launching or exploration.
items.best_for: A brief historical or engineering beat where a real mission-control texture matters.
items.label: NASA Apollo countdown or mission control call

items.avoid_when: The episode already has enough atmosphere from music or narration.
items.best_for: A moment that needs the listener to feel a remote environment, not just hear facts.
items.label: NASA Mars wind or rover environmental audio

items.avoid_when: The script would imply it is literal audible sound in space without explanation.
items.best_for: A demonstration beat about translating data into audible form.
items.label: NASA planetary radio emission sonification

items.avoid_when: No specific file has been chosen and attributed.
items.best_for: A short signal-processing or communication-history flourish.
items.credit: Credit per selected Wikimedia Commons file
items.label: Morse code signal
items.suggested_search: Wikimedia Commons Morse code audio CC BY

items.avoid_when: It would become a cute but distracting joke.
items.best_for: A timing/rhythm beat where an audible tick explains the concept faster than words.
items.credit: Credit per selected Wikimedia Commons file
items.label: Metronome tick

items.avoid_when: The final audio will already include generated music in the same frequency range.
items.best_for: A physics or music beat that benefits from hearing pitch stability.
items.credit: Credit per selected Wikimedia Commons file
items.label: Tuning fork tone

items.avoid_when: A file is CC BY-NC or license metadata is missing.
items.best_for: A real-world place or machine texture when a CC0 file is selected.
items.credit: Credit optional for CC0, but keep source metadata in manifest
items.label: CC0 field recording from Freesound
items.license_label: Must verify selected file is CC0

items.avoid_when: Rights metadata is unclear or the clip is merely decorative.
items.best_for: A historical episode where a specific archival recording is materially relevant.
items.credit: Credit per selected Internet Archive item
items.label: Public-domain archival audio from Internet Archive

policy.script_rule: Do not include sound-effect markup in spoken script text. The plan is an editorial/mixing note saved to the manifest.
policy.selection_rule: Use a sonic footnote only when it clarifies, demonstrates, or emotionally sharpens a specific beat. Silence is preferred over decoration.

sources_reviewed.name: NASA Images and Media Guidelines
sources_reviewed.note: NASA audio/media are generally not subject to US copyright, but NASA should be acknowledged and third-party marked material is excluded.

sources_reviewed.name: Wikimedia Commons licensing
sources_reviewed.note: Commons hosts public-domain and freely licensed media with per-file obligations.

sources_reviewed.name: Freesound licensing FAQ
sources_reviewed.note: Freesound includes CC0, CC BY, and CC BY-NC sounds; only CC0/CC BY are allowed here.

sources_reviewed.name: Internet Archive metadata
sources_reviewed.note: Internet Archive item-level licenseurl and rights fields must be checked before use.
